examples.title: Midpoint Circle Algorithm (Basic)
examples.description: Draws a circle centered at origin with radius r using Midpoint Circle Algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MidpointCircle(r)
BEGIN
 x ← 0
 y ← r
 p ← 1 - r // Initial decision parameter
 
 WHILE x < y DO
 // Plot points in all 8 octants
 plot(x, y)
 plot(y, x)
 plot(y, -x)
 plot(x, -y)
 plot(-x, -y)
 plot(-y, -x)
 plot(-y, x)
 plot(-x, y)
 
 IF p < 0 THEN
 // Midpoint is inside circle, choose E
 x ← x + 1
 p ← p + 2*x + 1
 ELSE
 // Midpoint is outside/on circle, choose SE
 x ← x + 1
 y ← y - 1
 p ← p + 2*x - 2*y + 1
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm starts at (0,r) and generates points by choosing between East and South-East pixels based on the decision parameter p. The 8-way symmetry is used to plot all points simultaneously.

examples.title: Midpoint Circle Algorithm with Center Offset
examples.description: Draws a circle centered at (h, k) with radius r
examples.code:
ALGORITHM MidpointCircleWithCenter(h, k, r)
BEGIN
 x ← 0
 y ← r
 p ← 1 - r
 
 WHILE x < y DO
 // Plot points in all 8 octants with center offset
 plot(x + h, y + k)
 plot(y + h, x + k)
 plot(y + h, -x + k)
 plot(x + h, -y + k)
 plot(-x + h, -y + k)
 plot(-y + h, -x + k)
 plot(-y + h, x + k)
 plot(-x + h, y + k)
 
 IF p < 0 THEN
 x ← x + 1
 p ← p + 2*x + 1
 ELSE
 x ← x + 1
 y ← y - 1
 p ← p + 2*x - 2*y + 1
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This version adds translation offsets (h, k) to all plotted points, allowing circles to be drawn at any position on the screen.

examples.title: Decision Parameter Update Derivation
examples.description: Shows mathematical derivation of p update formulas
examples.explanation: This shows the mathematical derivation of how the decision parameter is updated based on the circle equation and the position of the midpoint.
